title: Theory of Computation
description: Abstraction and decomposition, finite state machines and regular languages, the Turing machine, and the limits of what can be computed — including the halting problem and classifying problems as decidable/undecidable and tractable/intractable.
objectives: Apply abstraction and decomposition to break a problem down before solving it
Trace a finite state machine on a given input string to determine whether it accepts or rejects
Explain, conceptually, what a regular language is and what a simple regular expression can and cannot match
Describe the Turing machine (tape, head, states, transition rules) as a general model of computation
Explain, informally, why the halting problem is undecidable using the self-reference argument
Classify simple problems as decidable vs undecidable and tractable vs intractable

lessons.title: Regular languages and the Turing machine
The halting problem, decidability and tractability
